__NOTOC__ {{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |English Defence| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd| |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= | |pd| | | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | |pl| | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= |moves=1. d4 b6 |eco=[[Chess/ECOA|A40]] |parent=[[../|Queen's Pawn Opening]] }} == 1... b6 - English Defence == With the move '''1.... b6''' Black prepares to develop the Queenside Bishop. It's also a flexible option when played with the e6 development as Black has various ideas involving the e6, Nf6, Bb4, Bb7 moves. === White's responses === White likely to respond with one of the following: * '''2. c4''' A standard reply, White continues in Queen Gambit formation ready to play e4. * '''2. Nf3''' May transpose into a [[Chess/Queen's Indian Defence|Queen's Indian Defence]]. * '''2. e4''' This move order arrives at an [[Chess/Owen's Defence|Owen's Defence]]. == Theory table == {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}} '''1. d4 b6 '''
